volumeTitle: The Woman of Andros. The Self-Tormentor. The Eunuch
label: The Self-Tormentor (Section 4.8.842 - Section 5.1.894c)

sections.label: Section 4.8.842
sections.translation: My son , I now think myself the happiest of all men , since I find that you have returned to a rational mode of life .

sections.label: Section 4.8.844b
sections.translation: How much he is mistaken !

sections.label: Section 4.8.844c
sections.translation: Chremes , you are the very person I wanted ;

sections.label: Section 4.8.845
sections.original: Serva , quod in te est , filium et me et familiam .
sections.translation: preserve , so far as in you lies , my son , myself , and my family .

sections.label: Section 4.8.846
sections.translation: Tell me what you would have me do .

sections.label: Section 4.8.846b
sections.translation: You have this day found a daughter .

sections.label: Section 4.8.847
sections.translation: What then ?

sections.label: Section 4.8.847b
sections.original: Hanc sibi uxorem dari vult Clinia .
sections.translation: Clinia wishes her to be given him for a wife .

sections.label: Section 4.8.848
sections.translation: Prithee , what kind of a person are you ?

sections.label: Section 4.8.848b
sections.translation: Why ?

sections.label: Section 4.8.848c
sections.translation: Have you already forgotten what passed between us , concerning a scheme ,

sections.label: Section 4.8.850
sections.original: Ut ea via abs te argentum auferretur ?
sections.translation: that by that method some money might be got out of you ?

sections.label: Section 4.8.850b
sections.translation: I remember .

sections.label: Section 4.8.851
sections.translation: That self-same thing they are now about .

sections.label: Section 4.8.851b
sections.translation: What do you tell me , Chremes ? Why surely , this Courtesan , who is at my house , is Clitipho ’ s mistress .

sections.label: Section 4.8.853b
sections.original: Ita aiunt : et tu credis omnia :
sections.translation: So they say , and you believe it all ; and they say that he is desirous of a wife , in order that , when I have betrothed her ,

sections.label: Section 4.8.855
sections.original: Des qui aurum ac vestem atque alia quae opus sunt comparet .
sections.translation: you may give him money , with which to provide gold trinkets and clothing , and other things that are requisite .

sections.label: Section 4.8.856
sections.original: Id est profecto : id amicae dabitur .
sections.translation: That is it , no doubt ; that money will be given to his mistress .

sections.label: Section 4.8.856b
sections.translation: Of course it is to be given .

sections.label: Section 4.8.857b
sections.original: Vah , frustra sum igitur gavisus miser .
sections.translation: Alas ! in vain then , unhappy man , have I been overjoyed ; still however , I had rather any thing than be deprived of him . What answer now shall I report from you , Chremes ,

sections.label: Section 4.8.860
sections.original: Ne sentiat me sensisse , atque aegre ferat ?
sections.translation: so that he may not perceive that I have found it out , and take it to heart ?

sections.label: Section 4.8.861
sections.translation: To heart , indeed ! you are too indulgent to him , Menedemus .

sections.label: Section 4.8.861b
sections.translation: Let me go on ; I have now begun : assist me in this throughout , Chremes .

sections.label: Section 4.8.863
sections.original: Dic convenisse ; egisse te de nuptiis .
sections.translation: Say then , that you have seen me , and have treated about the marriage .

sections.label: Section 4.8.864
sections.translation: I ’ ll say so — what then ?

sections.label: Section 4.8.864b
sections.translation: That I will do every thing ;

sections.label: Section 4.8.865
sections.original: Generum placere : postremo etiam , si voles ,
sections.translation: that as a son-in-law he meets my approbation ; in fine , too , if you like , tell him also that she has been promised him .

sections.label: Section 4.8.866b
sections.translation: Well , that ’ s what I wanted —

sections.label: Section 4.8.867
sections.original: Tanto ocius te ut poscat , et tu id quod cupis
sections.translation: That he may the sooner ask of you , and you may as soon as possible give him what you wish .

sections.label: Section 4.8.868b
sections.translation: It is my wish .

sections.label: Section 4.8.868c
sections.translation: Assuredly , before very long , according as I view this matter , you ’ ll have enough of him .

sections.label: Section 4.8.870
sections.original: Sed haec ut ut sunt , cautim et paulatim dabis ,
sections.translation: But , however that may be , if you are wise , you ’ ll give to him cautiously , and a little at a time .

sections.label: Section 4.8.871b
sections.translation: I ’ ll do so .

sections.label: Section 4.8.871c
sections.translation: Go in-doors and see how much he requires . I shall be at home , if you should want me for any thing .

sections.label: Section 4.8.872b
sections.translation: I certainly do want you ; for I shall let you know whatever I do .

sections.label: Section 5.1.874
sections.original: Ego me non tam astutum , neque ita perspicacem esse certo scio :
sections.translation: I am quite aware that I am not so overwise , or so very quick-sighted ;

sections.label: Section 5.1.875
sections.original: Sed hic adiutor meus et monitor et praemonstrator Chremes
sections.translation: but this assistant , prompter , and director [*] of mine , Chremes , outdoes me in that . Any one of those epithets which are applied to a fool is suited to myself , such as dolt , post , ass , [*] lump of lead ; to him not one can apply ; his stupidity surpasses them all .

sections.label: Section 5.1.879
sections.original: Ohe , iam desine Deos , uxor , gratulando obtundere ,
sections.translation: Hold now , do , wife , leave off dinning the Gods with thanksgivings

sections.label: Section 5.1.880
sections.original: Tuam esse inventam gnatam ; nisi illos ex tuo ingenio iudicas ,
sections.translation: that your daughter has been discovered ; unless you judge of them by your own disposition , and think that they understand nothing , unless the same thing has been told them a hundred times . But , in the mean time , why does my son linger there so long with Syrus ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.883
sections.translation: What persons do you say are lingering ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.883b
sections.translation: Ha ! Menedemus , you have come opportunely . Tell me , have you told Clinia what I said ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.884b
sections.translation: Every thing .

sections.label: Section 5.1.885
sections.translation: What did he say ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.885b
sections.original: Gaudere adeo coepit quasi qui cupiunt nuptias .
sections.translation: He began to rejoice , just like people do who wish to be married .

sections.label: Section 5.1.886
sections.translation: Ha ! ha ! ha !

sections.label: Section 5.1.886b
sections.translation: Why are you laughing ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.886c
sections.translation: The sly tricks of my servant , Syrus , just came into my mind .

sections.label: Section 5.1.887b
sections.translation: Did they ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.887c
sections.translation: The rogue can even mould the countenances of people . [*]

sections.label: Section 5.1.888
sections.original: Gnatus quod se adsimulat laetum , id dicis ?
sections.translation: That my son is pretending that he is overjoyed , is it that you mean ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.888b
sections.translation: Just so .

sections.label: Section 5.1.888c
sections.translation: The very same thing came into my mind .

sections.label: Section 5.1.889b
sections.translation: A crafty knave !

sections.label: Section 5.1.889c
sections.translation: Still more would you think such to be the fact , if you knew more .

sections.label: Section 5.1.890b
sections.translation: Do you say so ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.890c
sections.translation: Do you give attention then ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.890d
sections.translation: Just stop — first I want to know this , what money you have squandered ; for when you told your son that she was promised , of course Dromo would at once throw in a word that golden jewels , clothes , and attendants would be needed for the bride , in order that you might give the money .

sections.label: Section 5.1.894
sections.translation: No .

sections.label: Section 5.1.894b
sections.translation: How , no ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.894c
sections.translation: No , I tell you .
